BUT live LTP data — first available this session at this assessment cadence — reveals (i) book is NET POSITIVE by +0.35% despite 3 OPEN orders unfilled, (ii) MAHABANK has fully recovered from its Day-3 peak distress, (iii) PNBHOUSING is the breakout winner of the day at +7.05%, (iv) strategy is silently maintaining SL hygiene on at least 3 names. The cap-induced silence narrative (from 13:30) holds: with the 0.65 deployed cap binding 7.53 pp under full-fill projection and three OPEN orders sitting aged at limit prices, no organic admission path exists. Risk level held at MEDIUM, NOT lowered to LOW because: (a) two OPEN orders past their age-flag thresholds is unfinished structural work, (b) deployment cap remains binding, (c) Power cluster 4-name structure persists at 2.28 pp buffer, (d) Trigger S posture-review fires this tick (the conditional has fully met), and (e) one tick of positive MTM does not constitute a regime change.

HOWEVER: the risk_monitor's role mandate is ONLY-TIGHTEN per agent definition. Relaxation of max_deployed_pct 0.65 → 0.68 is OUTSIDE this agent's authority. NO LEVER ACTION TAKEN. Escalation handed off: this finding flagged for the strategy-layer post-market review (strategy agent CAN evaluate relaxation per role parity with the 04-22 14:30 max_deployed_pct fire that strategy-agent ratified at 16:00 EOD). Documenting Trigger S as 'fired-no-action' rather than ignoring it preserves the trigger discipline — the conditional met cleanly; only the lever direction is out-of-mandate.

actions_taken: NO LEVER CHANGES. All seven active overrides held at 12:30 levels: min_confidence=0.65, max_position_pct=0.15, max_deployed_pct=0.65, default_sl_pct=0.015, min_sl_pct=0.01, max_sl_pct=0.04, max_sector_pct=0.22. Zero pre-armed TIGHTENING triggers fired in this 30-min window — third consecutive zero-tightening tick. Tightening levers in the absence of a firing trigger would be theatrical, would deepen admission friction without addressing any live risk vector, and would conflict with the Trigger S finding that the current cap is *already* over-restrictive (cap-induced entry silence confirmed, 105-min duration).

TRIGGER S FIRED → NO ACTION (out-of-mandate). Trigger S spec from 13:30: 'fire POSTURE REVIEW at 14:00 tick: assess whether 0.65 deployment cap is over-restrictive (consider relaxation back to 0.68 or 0.70).' All preconditions met cleanly: (a) entry/exit silence ≥105 min ✓ (105 min observed), (b) cap gap stays binding ✓ (7.53 pp), (c) no OPEN orders fill ✓ (3-of-3 still pending). Posture review concludes: silence IS structurally cap-induced (live SL-trailing data confirms decision-layer health — strategy is actively maintaining stops while admission window is closed). The prescribed action is RELAXATION (0.65 → 0.68 or 0.70). The risk_monitor agent's role is ONLY-TIGHTEN per system mandate — relaxation is OUTSIDE scope. Documenting Trigger S as 'fired-no-action / out-of-mandate' rather than silently ignoring it preserves trigger discipline. ESCALATION HANDED OFF: flagged for strategy_layer review (strategy agent has prior precedent for ratifying / unwinding risk_monitor overrides at EOD — see 04-22 16:00 EOD strategy review and 04-23 16:00 max_sl_pct fire). At the next strategy_review cycle, recommend strategy assesses whether the 0.65 cap should be relaxed.

Risk level HELD at MEDIUM (not raised to HIGH or CRITICAL — Trigger D dormant on all legs; not lowered to LOW — see drivers below). Drivers for HOLD: (i) book-positive +0.35% and MAHABANK recovery favor LOW, but (ii) two OPEN orders past their age-flag thresholds (TATAPOWER 227 min, HINDZINC 197 min) is unfinished structural work, (iii) deployment cap remains binding 7.53 pp under projection, (iv) Power cluster 4-name structure persists at 2.28 pp buffer, (v) Trigger S firing at this tick signals current overrides may be over-restrictive (a non-LOW-risk diagnostic), (vi) NESTLEIND SL buffer at 39 bps is tightest in the book and warrants observation. One favorable book-day does not constitute a regime change; need at least 2 confirming ticks before LOW.
